---
title: "References"
---

## Core methods

- **Lee & Schuler (2025).** *RieszBoost: Gradient Boosting for Riesz Regression.* [arXiv:2501.04871](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.04871) — the algorithm `rieszboost` implements.
- **Singh (2021).** *Kernel Methods for Unobserved Confounding: Negative Controls, Proxies, and Instruments.* [arXiv:2102.11076](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.11076) — closed-form RKHS estimator that `krrr` extends to the full set of estimands.

## Riesz regression background

- **Williams, Hines & Rudolph (2025).** *Riesz representers for the rest of us.* [arXiv:2507.19413](https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.19413) — accessible introduction to the Riesz representation theorem and its role in semiparametric efficient estimation.
- **Chernozhukov, Newey, Quintas-Martínez & Syrgkanis (2021).** *RieszNet and ForestRiesz: Automatic Debiased Machine Learning of Causal and Structural Effects.* [arXiv:2110.03031](https://arxiv.org/abs/2110.03031) — neural-net and random-forest Riesz regression.
- **Chernozhukov, Newey, Quintas-Martínez & Syrgkanis (2022).** *Automatic Debiased Machine Learning via Riesz Regression.* [arXiv:2104.14737](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.14737) — origin of the squared Riesz loss.

## Bregman generalization

- **Hines & Miles (2025).** *Bregman-divergence Riesz Regression.* [arXiv:2510.16127](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.16127).
- **Kato (2026).** *Generalized Riesz Loss via Bregman Divergence.* [arXiv:2601.07752](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.07752).

## Beyond linear functionals

- **van der Laan, Luedtke & van der Laan (2025).** *Automatic Doubly-Robust Estimation for Smooth Functionals of the Outcome Mechanism.* [arXiv:2501.11868](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.11868) — auto-DML for smooth functionals beyond linear.

## Solver foundations (krrr)

- **Rudi, Carratino & Rosasco (2017).** *FALKON: An Optimal Large Scale Kernel Method.* [arXiv:1705.10958](https://arxiv.org/abs/1705.10958) — Nyström + preconditioned CG, optional GPU backend.
- **Rahimi & Recht (2007).** *Random Features for Large-Scale Kernel Machines.* NIPS — basis of the random Fourier features solver.

## Semiparametric estimation context

- **Hubbard, van der Laan & Robins (2011).** *Estimation of the Causal Effects of Time-Varying Exposures.* The delta-method-EIF reference for partial-parameter estimands like ATT and LASE.

## Software

- **Lee's reference implementation.** [github.com/kaitlynjlee/boosting_for_rr](https://github.com/kaitlynjlee/boosting_for_rr) — `rieszboost` cross-checks against this. See [`rieszboost/examples/lee_schuler/COMPARISON.md`](https://github.com/rieszreg/rieszreg/blob/main/rieszboost/examples/lee_schuler/COMPARISON.md) for the parity report.
- **dml-tmle.** [github.com/alejandroschuler/dml-tmle](https://github.com/alejandroschuler/dml-tmle) — R reference implementation for TSM1 kernel ridge; `krrr` parity-tests against it at 1e-8.

The full set of papers (with arXiv source archives) lives at [`reference/`](https://github.com/rieszreg/rieszreg/tree/main/reference) at the top level of the repo.
